history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade due to HttpStatus.is4xxClientError API signature change in Spring Framework 6.
history.qwen_rationale: The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe updated dependencies but missed migrating the HttpStatus.is4xxClientError(HttpStatusCode) call to the new instance method. Explicitly running UpgradeSpringFramework_6_1 applies targeted Spring Framework 6.1 API migration recipes that fix this and related breaking changes.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed because Spring Framework 6.0 (bundled with Spring Boot 3.0+) removed the static method HttpStatus.is4xxClientError(HttpStatusCode), and the monolithic UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 recipe did not successfully refactor the call before the post-recipe compile check.
history.qwen_rationale: Splitting the Spring Boot upgrade into incremental steps (2.7 -> 3.0 -> 3.1 -> 3.2 -> 3.3) ensures that each version's targeted migration recipes run and compile successfully before proceeding. UpgradeSpringBoot_3_0 specifically addresses Spring Framework 6.0 API changes, including the HttpStatus static method removal, preventing the compilation failure that halted the previous monolithic step.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed at the Spring Boot 3.0 step due to an unhandled Spring Framework 6 API change where HttpStatus.is4xxClientError() signature changed, breaking the method reference in ApplicationConfig.java.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing the incremental Spring Boot upgrade steps with a single UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 meta-recipe step ensures all Spring 6 code transformations (including HttpStatus and WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter migrations) are applied atomically before compilation. Adding UpgradeSpringFramework_6_1 explicitly covers any remaining framework-level API shifts that the boot meta-recipe might miss.

history.qwen_observation: The UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 recipe left javax.validation:validation-api without a version, causing a Maven POM parsing error since Spring Boot 3.x parent no longer manages the javax namespace.
history.qwen_rationale: Explicitly setting the version for javax.validation:validation-api resolves the POM error, and adding the JavaxValidationMigrationToJakartaValidation recipe ensures the dependency and imports are correctly migrated to the jakarta namespace required by Spring Boot 3.x.

history.qwen_rationale: Removing the problematic dependency upgrade mapping and relying on the JavaxValidationMigrationToJakartaValidation recipe avoids the validation error. The chain proceeds with the Spring Boot 3.3 meta-recipe, framework upgrade, and standard Java 21 migration steps.
